authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: Workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes the official Actions workflow model for repository-hosted CI/CD automation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pipeline intake, design, and workflow evidence review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow concepts, runner behavior, or syntax.

authoritative_sources.title: Workflow syntax for GitHub Actions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes the official syntax reference for workflow keys, permissions, concurrency, jobs, needs, environments, and conditions.
practical_use_in_pack: reusable workflow and environment gate placement
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes supported workflow syntax.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: Deployment environments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub defines deployment environment gates, protection rules, approvals, and environment-scoped secrets used as delivery controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold/release boundary and deployment approval review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes deployment environment behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Secure use reference for GitHub Actions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official security guidance for safe Actions usage, untrusted input, third-party actions, secrets, and runner trust.
practical_use_in_pack: secret exposure and untrusted pull request checks
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes Actions security guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: OpenID Connect
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub defines its standards-based OIDC federation behavior for workflow identity and short-lived external provider access.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes OIDC claims, token behavior, or deployment guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: GitLab CI/CD pipelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitLab publishes the official pipeline model for GitLab CI/CD, including jobs, stages, needs, artifacts, and child pipelines.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: GitLab pipeline design and portability review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on GitLab release changes.

authoritative_sources.title: GitLab protected environments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitLab publishes the official protected-environment model used to restrict deployments to approved people, groups, or roles.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitLab changes protected environment behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: GitLab merge request approvals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitLab publishes official merge request approval behavior used to distinguish technical review support from final approval authority.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitLab changes merge request approval behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Jenkins Pipeline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Jenkins Project publishes the official Pipeline handbook for Pipeline as code, Jenkinsfile source control, stages, steps, input, and audit trail.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Jenkinsfile design, inspection, and evidence review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Jenkins Pipeline documentation or LTS behavior materially changes.

authoritative_sources.title: Using a Jenkinsfile
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Jenkins Project publishes official guidance for source-controlled Jenkinsfiles, build/test/deploy stages, credentials, parameters, and failure handling.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Jenkinsfile guidance changes.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218: Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is a U.S. national secure software development framework covering security practices, evidence, and vulnerability response.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure pipeline control and remediation review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on NIST revision or new federal secure software guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP CI/CD Security Cheat Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP publishes recognized public guidance specifically for CI/CD risks, secure configuration, IAM, secrets, third-party code, integrity, and visibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI/CD security review and failure-mode handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OWASP cheat sheet update.

authoritative_sources.title: SLSA v1.1 Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SLSA is an industry-consensus framework for supply-chain security levels, build integrity, and provenance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: provenance and artifact integrity gate review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when SLSA publishes a new specification version.

authoritative_sources.title: CycloneDX Specification Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CycloneDX is a public SBOM and supply-chain transparency specification used for dependency and evidence artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SBOM evidence and release package review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when CycloneDX publishes a new specification version.

authoritative_sources.title: Open Container Initiative Image Format Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OCI defines the container image manifest, layers, descriptors, configuration, and content-addressable identity used in common pipeline artifact flows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: container artifact and immutable promotion review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when OCI image-spec releases or main specification changes materially.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About pull request reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official pull request review behavior used to separate review evidence, requested changes, approvals, and final merge authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review evidence, approval boundary, and resubmission tracking
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, privacy, confidentiality, harm avoidance, truthful claims, and professional judgment boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality, client delivery, and professional judgment boundary
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

authoritative_sources.title: Software Engineering Code of Ethics and Professional Practice
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The ACM/IEEE Computer Society code is a recognized professional framework for software engineering public interest, client/employer obligations, product quality, judgment, and authority boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: professional-services quality, client commitment, and authority boundary
practical_use_in_pack: client rights and professional judgment escalation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM/IEEE Computer Society code revision.

workflow_stages.name: engagement and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm request type, repository scope, workflow platform, build graph, tenant facts, systems of record, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, source freshness, and expected output shape before assessing the pipeline.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: pipeline artifact and control review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect workflow definitions, stages, jobs, runner model, secrets, permissions, caches, artifacts, tests, scans, approvals, environment gates, and deployment records against correctness, maintainability, security, artifact integrity, and release-safety criteria.
source_classes: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
Public security and software quality guidance
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability

workflow_stages.name: quality, security, and release disposition
workflow_stages.description: Classify evidence as satisfied, failed, unclear, blocked, hold, or release-ready recommendation without granting final release authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: defect, incident, and corrective-action handling
workflow_stages.description: Convert failed runs, recurring defects, flaky tests, deployment incidents, artifact gaps, and nonconformances into incident summaries, remediation plans, rerun criteria, and escalation packets.
source_classes: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
Public security and software quality guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: delivery, confidentiality, and signoff gating
workflow_stages.description: Before packaging a client or audit output, check recipient authorization, confidentiality, tenant policy, engagement scope, contractual commitments, approval authority, and professional judgment boundaries.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: request, acceptance criteria, engagement scope, confidentiality terms, client commitments, tenant policy, and approval matrix

repository layout, build graph, branch model, merge rules, workflow definitions, GitHub Actions workflows, GitLab CI/CD configuration, Jenkinsfiles, reusable workflows, shared libraries, and pipeline templates
language/runtime/tool version constraints, dependency lockfiles, cache keys, runner labels, container build metadata, environment topology, and deployment configuration
workflow run IDs, job logs, test reports, scan outputs, SBOMs, artifact digests, container images, signatures, provenance records, attestations, and release notes

protected environment approvals, merge or pull request reviews, deployment records, rollback plans, health checks, observability dashboards, incident records, defect tickets, remediation trackers, and reviewer responses

pipeline blueprint, quality or safety review note, control or incident summary, corrective-action escalation, release-readiness checklist, rollback recommendation, evidence checklist, and confidentiality-safe delivery note
systems_of_record: source repository and pull request or merge request system
CI/CD platform record for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or tenant-approved equivalent
workflow run history, job logs, test report store, scan report store, cache metadata, artifact registry, container registry, and package registry
SBOM store, signing key or identity records, artifact attestation or provenance registry, release notes, and deployment evidence store
environment protection settings, approval history, change/request record, issue tracker, incident tracker, defect tracker, remediation tracker, and review-note register
observability platform for logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, health checks, alerts, and SLO evidence
tenant document management system, engagement scope, confidentiality records, client commitments, and approval matrix

allowed: recommend CI/CD workflow designs, job/stage structures, trust boundaries, cache and artifact controls, test and scan gates, provenance gates, rollout checks, rollback criteria, and review findings
classify pipeline evidence as satisfied, failed, unclear, blocked, hold, or release-ready recommendation for review support
request missing artifacts and return low-confidence cases to orchestrator
route adjacent or authority-bound work to the correct owner
escalate: release approval, merge approval, contractual acceptance, legal/compliance interpretation, audit attestation, client rights, or licensed/professional judgment is required
tenant policy conflicts with public source guidance or confidentiality terms change the allowed output
critical repository, workflow run, log, report, artifact, deployment, incident, or approval evidence is missing or contradictory

security risk acceptance, enterprise IAM, SOC implementation, platform operations, runner fleet provisioning, SRE incident command, production rollback execution, or network/cloud operations are required
refuse: requests for production infrastructure provisioning, tuning, privileged deployment execution, or live incident operations
requests for organizational hiring strategy, staffing plans, product roadmap, pricing, or go-to-market strategy
requests for security architecture outside the CI/CD execution path, IAM program design, SOC implementation, legal/compliance interpretation, attestation, or final signoff
requests to bypass protected environments, manual approvals, required reviews, quality gates, release holds, or documented safety controls without explicit authority
requests to expose confidential tenant/client artifacts, logs, scan reports, or delivery evidence to unapproved recipients

domain_failure_modes: generic CI/CD advice that is not tied to provided workflow files, run IDs, logs, test reports, scan outputs, artifacts, deployment records, or tenant systems of record
unsupported clearance of a hold, release gate, audit package, review finding, or defect without remediation and approval evidence
pipeline recommendations that assume privileged runner, cloud, secret, or deployment access not supplied by tenant authority
over-authoritative legal, compliance, security-risk, attestation, contractual, or final release approval claims
confidential client or tenant logs, scan reports, incident notes, or delivery records sent to an unapproved recipient or summarized beyond approved scope
security guidance that turns into enterprise security architecture, IAM program design, SOC implementation, or risk acceptance
platform operations, SRE incident command, product strategy, staffing, or application implementation work retained instead of routed
tenant-specific policy conflicts confidently resolved without approval owner review
artifact integrity, SBOM, provenance, signing, or digest evidence treated as present when source records are missing

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
security and quality expectations where applicable
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.
Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding.
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack uses public GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, security, artifact, and professional sources and records that tenant-supplied platform versions, policies, and approval rules must be retrieved before stack-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal, compliance, attestation, contractual, release, and licensed engineering authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack refuses final signoff and routes authority-bound questions to human owners or orchestrator.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Production execution and incident command authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack provides pipeline guidance and handoff evidence only; production operation, rollback execution, runner provisioning, and SRE incident control route out of lane.
